parameters.name: startKey
parameters.description: Key for pagination, indicating where to start the list

parameters.name: search
parameters.description: keyword search for the users (could be name, or email)

parameters.name: factor.id
parameters.description:
for OTPs this will be a request_id, for email_password this
will be the email address

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: ID of the user to fetch

parameters.name: userId
parameters.description: ID of the user fetching the farmer

parameters.name: farmerId
parameters.description: ID of the farmer to be fetched

secretValue.title: Value of the factor (e.g., password or OTP)

contentType.description: The HTTP Content-Type header value specifying the content type of the body.

data.description: The HTTP request/response body as raw binary.

extensions.description:
Application specific response metadata. Must be set in the first response
for streaming APIs.

@type.description:
A URL/resource name that uniquely identifies the type of the serialized
protocol buffer message. This string must contain at least
one "/" character. The last segment of the URL's path must represent
the fully qualified name of the type (as in
`path/google.protobuf.Duration`). The name should be in a canonical form
(e.g., leading "." is not accepted).

In practice, teams usually precompile into the binary all types that they
expect it to use in the context of Any. However, for URLs which use the
scheme `http`, `https`, or no scheme, one can optionally set up a type
server that maps type URLs to message definitions as follows:

* If no scheme is provided, `https` is assumed.
* An HTTP GET on the URL must yield a [google.protobuf.Type][]
  value in binary format, or produce an error.
* Applications are allowed to cache lookup results based on the
  URL, or have them precompiled into a binary to avoid any
  lookup. Therefore, binary compatibility needs to be preserved
  on changes to types. (Use versioned type names to manage
  breaking changes.)

Note: this functionality is not currently available in the official
protobuf release, and it is not used for type URLs beginning with
type.googleapis.com.

Schemes other than `http`, `https` (or the empty scheme) might be
used with implementation specific semantics.

requestId.title: ID for the additional factor request"security_question"

factorHint.title: Hint for the additional factor (e.g., last 4 digits of phone number)

usersgrpcAdditionalFactor.description: Request and response messages for gRPC methods.

id.title:
for OTPs this will be a request_id, for email_password this
will be the email address

secretValue.title: Value of the factor (e.g., password or OTP)

factors.title: A list of authentication factors provided by the client

loginComplete.title:
Indicates if login is complete
Returned if an additional factor is required

tokens.title: Access and refresh tokens returned if login is complete

nextKey.title: Key for the next set of results (for pagination)

usersgrpcOtpIntent.title:
- OTP_INTENT_UNSPECIFIED: Default value if no intent is specified
 - OTP_INTENT_LOGIN: OTP for login
 - OTP_INTENT_RESET_PASSWORD: OTP for resetting password
 - OTP_INTENT_SIGNUP: OTP for signing up
 - OTP_INTENT_VERIFY_EMAIL: OTP for verifying email

refreshToken.title: The refresh token to validate and exchange for a new access token
Token to refresh access when expired

valid.title: returns true if the otp matches the request, else returns false
